document.id: Pueblo of Picuris Tribal Council Res. No. 2026-10
document.codify:doc: Pueblo of Picuris Tribal Law and Order Code
document.heading: A RESOLUTION OF THE GOVERNING BODY OF PICURIS PUEBLO TO STRENGTHEN ENFORCEMENT AND PENALTIES FOR LITTERING AND ILLEGAL DUMPING WITHIN THE EXTERIOR BOUNDARIES AND CHECKERBOARD AREAS OF THE PUEBLO; TO ESTABLISH A SCALED CIVIL PENALTY STRUCTURE; AND TO DIRECT UPDATED PUBLIC NOTICE AND SIGNAGE

text.strong: The Pueblo of Picuris exercises inherent sovereign authority to protect its lands, natural resources, public health, and welfare within its exterior boundaries; and
The Pueblo's lands, including checkerboard areas, are experiencing increased littering and illegal dumping causing environmental, cultural, and public health harm; and
The current civil penalty of $300 is outdated and insufficient to deter violations; and
The Pueblo has a compelling interest in strengthening enforcement mechanisms; and
Public awareness and updated signage are necessary to support enforcement and compliance;

section.heading: Updated Civil Penalties
section.text: Third and Subsequent Offenses: Not less than $5,000
Each day a violation continues may be treated as a separate offense at the discretion of the Tribal Court.

section.heading: Aggravating Factors and Enhanced Penalties
section.text: Penalties may exceed $5,000 and include full restitution.

section.heading: Jurisdiction and Enforcement
section.text: Applies to all persons within the exterior boundaries to the fullest extent of Tribal and federal law.
Enforced by Tribal law enforcement and authorized Pueblo officials.

section.heading: Signage and Public Notice
section.text: The Pueblo shall install updated signage stating:

Minimum $1,000 fine
Escalating penalties
Subject to Tribal Court jurisdiction

section.heading: Severability
section.text: If any provision is invalid, remaining provisions remain in effect.

procedural-data.text: I, the undersigned, as Governor of the Pueblo of Picuris, hereby, certify that the Pueblo of Picuris Tribal Council, at a duly called meeting convened on the 13th day of March, 2026, at the Pueblo of Picuris, at which a quorum was present, approved the foregoing Resolution, and that 6 voted for, 0 were absent, and 2 were opposed.
